config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters a lot, but the rights of the individual should not be treated as secondary by default, because that is how abuse and injustice often start. In my view, a society is healthiest when it protects personal dignity, freedom, and due process while still asking people to contribute to the common good. Collective interests can justify some limits, but only narrowly and with strong safeguards.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya di Amerika Serikat, sejarah kami menunjukkan bahwa ketika negara atau mayoritas diberi terlalu banyak kuasa atas individu, penyalahgunaan mudah terjadi, terutama terhadap kelompok minoritas. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar seperti kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum yang adil, dan perlindungan dari tindakan sewenang-wenang. Jadi, yang paling masuk akal menurut saya adalah menyeimbangkan keduanya, bukan menempatkan individu sebagai korban default demi “kepentingan bersama.”

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I disagree with the statement because prioritizing society over the individual too easily becomes an excuse for the majority or the state to trample people’s dignity. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how appeals to harmony, order, or the common good can be used to silence criticism, protect the powerful, or pressure minorities to stay quiet. Society does matter, but it is strongest when individual rights are protected first as a baseline, with limits only when there is a clear and necessary public reason. If we reverse that order, the people who lose are usually the ones with the least power to defend themselves.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman saya di Amerika Serikat, alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pelanggaran kebebasan berbicara, diskriminasi, atau tindakan pemerintah yang berlebihan, dan itu biasanya paling merugikan kelompok minoritas. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi hak dasar sambil tetap mengatur kepentingan publik secara sempit dan terukur. Jadi, prioritasnya seharusnya bukan masyarakat dulu baru individu, melainkan hak individu sebagai batas utama bagi tindakan kolektif.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I reject the statement because in practice “society first” often becomes a blank check for the majority, officials, or local power brokers to silence people. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how appeals to harmony, order, and the common good can pressure dissenters and minorities to stay quiet, so I do not trust that phrase unless it is tightly limited. Society is important, but it is healthiest when individual rights are the baseline and collective action must justify any restriction clearly and narrowly. If we start by putting society above the person, the people who suffer first are usually those with the least protection.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat terlalu sering alasan “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk membenarkan pelanggaran kebebasan berbicara, diskriminasi, atau perluasan kuasa negara yang berlebihan, dan yang paling sering kena dampaknya justru kelompok minoritas. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan orang, melainkan yang menjadikan hak dasar sebagai batas utama bagi tindakan kolektif. Kepentingan publik tetap penting, tetapi hanya sah jika pembatasannya sempit, perlu, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
